properties.query:
// Legacy table view (apifirewall_log_1_CL - CLv1 with type-suffix columns)
// Handles customers who have NOT yet migrated to the CCF push connector.
let apifirewall_log_1_CL_view = view () {
    apifirewall_log_1_CL
    | extend
        Uuid            = tostring(column_ifexists('UUID_g', '')),
        Timestamp       = column_ifexists('Timestamp_t', datetime(null)),
        InstanceName    = column_ifexists('Instance_Name_s', ''),
        Hostname        = column_ifexists('Hostname_s', ''),
        SourceIp        = column_ifexists('Source_IP_s', ''),
        SourcePort      = toint(column_ifexists('Source_Port_d', real(null))),
        DestinationPort = toint(column_ifexists('Destination_Port_d', real(null))),
        UriPath         = column_ifexists('URI_Path_s', ''),
        Query           = column_ifexists('Query_s', ''),
        Status          = toint(column_ifexists('Status_d', real(null))),
        ErrorMessage    = column_ifexists('Error_Message_s', ''),
        Errors          = column_ifexists('Errors_s', ''),
        RequestHeader   = column_ifexists('Request_Header_s', ''),
        ResponseHeader  = column_ifexists('Response_Header_s', ''),
        ApiId           = tostring(column_ifexists('API_ID_g', '')),
        NonBlockingMode = column_ifexists('Non_blocking_mode_b', bool(null)),
        EventType       = column_ifexists('Type', '')
    | project
        TimeGenerated,
        Uuid,
        Timestamp,
        InstanceName,
        Hostname,
        SourceIp,
        SourcePort,
        DestinationPort,
        UriPath,
        Query,
        Status,
        ErrorMessage,
        Errors,
        RequestHeader,
        ResponseHeader,
        ApiId,
        NonBlockingMode,
        EventType
};
// New CCF table view (FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2_CL - CLv2 with PascalCase columns)
// Handles customers who have migrated to the CCF push connector.
let FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2_CL_view = view () {
    FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2_CL
    | extend
        Uuid            = column_ifexists('Uuid', ''),
        Timestamp       = column_ifexists('Timestamp', datetime(null)),
        InstanceName    = column_ifexists('InstanceName', ''),
        Hostname        = column_ifexists('Hostname', ''),
        SourceIp        = column_ifexists('SourceIp', ''),
        SourcePort      = column_ifexists('SourcePort', int(null)),
        DestinationPort = column_ifexists('DestinationPort', int(null)),
        UriPath         = column_ifexists('UriPath', ''),
        Query           = column_ifexists('Query', ''),
        Status          = column_ifexists('Status', int(null)),
        ErrorMessage    = column_ifexists('ErrorMessage', ''),
        Errors          = column_ifexists('Errors', ''),
        RequestHeader   = column_ifexists('RequestHeader', ''),
        ResponseHeader  = column_ifexists('ResponseHeader', ''),
        ApiId           = column_ifexists('ApiId', ''),
        NonBlockingMode = column_ifexists('NonBlockingMode', bool(null)),
        EventType       = column_ifexists('EventType', '')
    | project
        TimeGenerated,
        Uuid,
        Timestamp,
        InstanceName,
        Hostname,
        SourceIp,
        SourcePort,
        DestinationPort,
        UriPath,
        Query,
        Status,
        ErrorMessage,
        Errors,
        RequestHeader,
        ResponseHeader,
        ApiId,
        NonBlockingMode,
        EventType
};
union isfuzzy=true apifirewall_log_1_CL_view, FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2_CL_view

properties.description: 42Crunch API Protection data connector with template version 3.0.1

connectorUiConfig.title: API Protection
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Connects the 42Crunch API protection to Azure Log Analytics via the REST API interface

sampleQueries.query:
apifirewall_log_1_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where Status_d == 429

sampleQueries.description: API requests generating a server error
sampleQueries.query:
apifirewall_log_1_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where Status_d >= 500 and Status_d <= 599

apifirewall_log_1_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where Error_Message_s contains "missing [\"x-access-token\"]"

dataTypes.name: apifirewall_log_1_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
apifirewall_log_1_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
apifirewall_log_1_CL
| summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
 | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(7d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

instructionSteps.description: The installation process is documented in great detail in the GitHub repository [Microsoft Sentinel integration](https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration). The user should consult this repository further to understand installation and debug of the integration.
instructionSteps.title: Step 1 : Read the detailed documentation

instructionSteps.description:
The first installation step is to retrieve both your **Workspace ID** and **Primary Key** from the Microsoft Sentinel platform.
Copy the values shown below and save them for configuration of the API log forwarder integration.
instructionSteps.title: Step 2: Retrieve the workspace access credentials

instructionSteps.description: The next step is to install the 42Crunch protection and log forwarder to protect your API. Both components are availabe as containers from the [42Crunch repository](https://hub.docker.com/u/42crunch). The exact installation will depend on your environment, consult the [42Crunch protection documentation](https://docs.42crunch.com/latest/content/concepts/api_firewall_deployment_architecture.htm) for full details. Two common installation scenarios are described below:

innerSteps.title: Installation via Docker Compose
innerSteps.description: The solution can be installed using a [Docker compose file](https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration/blob/main/sample-deployment/docker-compose.yml).

innerSteps.title: Installation via Helm charts
innerSteps.description: The solution can be installed using a [Helm chart](https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration/tree/main/helm/sentinel).

instructionSteps.title: Step 3: Install the 42Crunch protection and log forwarder

instructionSteps.description: In order to test the data ingestion the user should deploy the sample *httpbin* application alongside the 42Crunch protection and log forwarder [described in detail here](https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration/tree/main/sample-deployment).

innerSteps.title: 4.1 Install the sample
innerSteps.description: The sample application can be installed locally using a [Docker compose file](https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration/blob/main/sample-deployment/docker-compose.yml) which will install the httpbin API server, the 42Crunch API protection and the Microsoft Sentinel log forwarder. Set the environment variables as required using the values copied from step 2.

innerSteps.title: 4.2 Run the sample
innerSteps.description: Verfify the API protection is connected to the 42Crunch platform, and then exercise the API locally on the *localhost* at port 8080 using Postman, curl, or similar. You should see a mixture of passing and failing API calls.

innerSteps.title: 4.3 Verify the data ingestion on Log Analytics
innerSteps.description: After approximately 20 minutes access the Log Analytics workspace on your Microsoft Sentinel installation, and locate the *Custom Logs* section verify that a *apifirewall_log_1_CL* table exists. Use the sample queries to examine the data.

instructionSteps.title: Step 4: Test the data ingestion

properties.displayName: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Connects the 42Crunch API protection to Microsoft Sentinel via the Azure Monitor DCE/DCR REST API interface. The integration uses Docker containers (API Firewall and Log Forwarder) to forward API traffic logs to Microsoft Sentinel for threat detection and analysis.

sampleQueries.description: API requests generating a server error
Top source IPs by request count
sampleQueries.query:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize RequestCount = count() by SourceIp
| top 10 by RequestCount desc

sampleQueries.description: API requests by HTTP status code
sampleQueries.query:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize Count = count() by Status
| order by Count desc

| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Insights/dataCollectionEndpoints
customs.description: Data Collection Endpoint is required for the modern push connector to receive data from the external security system.

customs.name: Microsoft.Insights/dataCollectionRules
customs.description: Data Collection Rule is required to define the schema and transformations for incoming data.

customs.name: Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments
customs.description: Role assignment is required to grant the Entra application permissions to send data to the Data Collection Rule.

customs.name: 42Crunch API Firewall requirement
customs.description: The 42Crunch API Firewall and Log Forwarder Docker containers must be deployed and running. Obtain your API Firewall token (42C_FIREWALL_TOKEN) and platform URL (42C_PLATFORM_URL) from the 42Crunch platform at https://platform.42crunch.com. Full deployment instructions are available at https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Create ARM Resources and Provide the Required Permissions
instructionSteps.description:
This connector receives data from external security systems that push logs to Microsoft Sentinel. The external system must be configured to send raw event data to the Microsoft Sentinel Ingestion API. Clicking on **Deploy** will trigger the creation of Log Analytics tables and a Data Collection Rule (DCR). It will then create an Entra application, link the DCR to it, and set the entered secret in the application. This setup enables data to be sent securely to the DCR using an Entra token.

[Learn more about the connector setup process](https://review.learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/create-push-codeless-connector)

parameters.content:
## Azure Resource Deployment

The deployment process will create the following resources:
- Log Analytics custom table(s)
- Data Collection Endpoint (DCE)
- Data Collection Rule (DCR)
- Entra ID Application Registration
- Role assignments for data ingestion

parameters.label: Deploy 42Crunch API Protection connector resources
parameters.applicationDisplayName: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)

parameters.content:
### Configuration Values

After deployment, copy the following values to configure your external security system:

parameters.text: Keep these values secure. You will need them to configure your external security system.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure your external system to push logs
instructionSteps.description:
Use the following parameters to configure your external security system to send logs to the workspace.

### Configuration Steps

1. Access your external security system's configuration interface.
2. Navigate to the data forwarding, integration, or SIEM settings.
3. Select **Azure Monitor** or **Microsoft Sentinel** as the destination.
4. Select **Data Collection Endpoint** as the authentication method.
5. Configure the required fields using the values from the previous step:
   - **Tenant ID**: Copy from above
   - **Application (Client) ID**: Copy from above
   - **Client Secret**: Copy from above
   - **Data Collection Endpoint**: Copy from above
   - **Data Collection Rule Immutable ID**: Copy from above
   - **Stream Name**: Copy the appropriate stream name from above
6. Save the configuration and enable/start the forwarder.

### Validation

> **Note**: Data will appear in the **FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2** table in your Log Analytics workspace within a few minutes.

To verify data is being received, run the following query in your Log Analytics workspace:

```kusto
FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 10
```

### Troubleshooting

If data is not appearing:
- Verify the configuration values are correct
- Check that the forwarder/integration is enabled and running
- Review logs in your external security system for any errors
- Ensure network connectivity from your system to Azure
- Verify the Entra application has the correct permissions

parameters.content: After deploying the Azure resources, configure the **42Crunch Log Forwarder** Docker container to send data to the deployed DCE/DCR endpoints instead of the legacy HTTP Data Collector API.

### Update Docker Compose / Helm Chart Environment Variables

Replace the legacy `WORKSPACE_ID` and `WORKSPACE_KEY` environment variables with the new DCE/DCR configuration:

```yaml
environment:
  # Legacy (remove these):
  # - WORKSPACE_ID=<workspace-id>
  # - WORKSPACE_KEY=<primary-key>

  # New CCF Push Connector configuration:
  - DCE_ENDPOINT=<data-collection-endpoint-url>
  - DCR_IMMUTABLE_ID=<data-collection-rule-immutable-id>
  - DCR_STREAM_NAME=Custom-FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2_CL

  # 42Crunch platform credentials (unchanged):
  - 42C_FIREWALL_TOKEN=<your-firewall-token>
  - 42C_PLATFORM_URL=<your-platform-url>
```

The DCE endpoint URL and DCR immutable ID are displayed after deploying the ARM resources in Step 1. For full configuration details, refer to the [42Crunch Microsoft Sentinel Integration](https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration) documentation.

### Important: Connection Status

This is a **push-based connector** - it receives data from the 42Crunch Log Forwarder and does not maintain an active polling connection. The connector shows as **Connected** when data has been received within the last 7 days.

instructionSteps.title: Uninstall connector
instructionSteps.description: Use this PowerShell script to delete the connector instance.

parameters.content: ### Delete the Connector Instance

Run the following PowerShell commands to remove the data connector instance:

```powershell
$sub = "<subscription-id>"
$rg  = "<resource-group-name>"
$ws  = "<workspace-name>"
$connectorName = "FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtection"
$armEndpoint = (az cloud show --query endpoints.resourceManager -o tsv).TrimEnd('/')

Write-Host "Deleting $connectorName..." -NoNewline
az rest --method DELETE --uri "$armEndpoint/subscriptions/$sub/resourceGroups/$rg/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/$ws/providers/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/dataConnectors/$connectorName`?api-version=2024-09-01"
Write-Host " Done" -ForegroundColor Green
```
> NOTE: Replace `<subscription-id>`, `<resource-group-name>`, and `<workspace-name>` with your actual Azure values before running the script.
This removes the push connector instance. Historical data in your Log Analytics workspace will be retained.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnull(Timestamp) or Timestamp < ago(2d), now(), Timestamp) | project TimeGenerated , Uuid , Timestamp , InstanceName , Hostname , SourceIp , SourcePort , DestinationPort , UriPath , Query , Status , ErrorMessage , Errors , RequestHeader , ResponseHeader , ApiId , NonBlockingMode , EventType

schema.name: FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2_CL
schema.description: 42Crunch API Firewall log events. Contains API request/response telemetry including source IP, URI paths, HTTP status codes, JWT validation errors, and security policy enforcement results.

columns.name: Uuid
columns.description: Unique identifier (UUID) for the log event

columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the API request recorded by the 42Crunch firewall

columns.name: InstanceName
columns.description: Name of the 42Crunch API Firewall instance that processed the request

columns.name: Hostname
columns.description: Hostname of the API server receiving the request

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: Source IP address of the API client

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: Source port number of the API client connection

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: Destination port number on the API server

columns.name: UriPath
columns.description: URI path of the API request

columns.name: Query
columns.description: Query string parameters of the API request

columns.name: Status
columns.description: HTTP response status code returned by the API firewall (e.g., 200, 401, 429, 500)

columns.name: ErrorMessage
columns.description: Error message returned by the API firewall when the request is blocked or fails validation

columns.name: Errors
columns.description: Detailed error information in JSON format from the API firewall

columns.name: RequestHeader
columns.description: HTTP request headers in JSON format

columns.name: ResponseHeader
columns.description: HTTP response headers in JSON format

columns.name: ApiId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the API definition in the 42Crunch platform

columns.name: NonBlockingMode
columns.description: Indicates whether the 42Crunch firewall is running in non-blocking (observation) mode. True means requests are logged but not blocked.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Type of the log event recorded by the API firewall (e.g., request, block, error)

connectorUiConfig.title: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Connects the 42Crunch API protection to Microsoft Sentinel via the Azure Monitor DCE/DCR REST API interface. The integration uses Docker containers (API Firewall and Log Forwarder) to forward API traffic logs to Microsoft Sentinel for threat detection and analysis.

sampleQueries.description: API requests generating a server error
Top source IPs by request count
sampleQueries.query:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize RequestCount = count() by SourceIp
| top 10 by RequestCount desc

sampleQueries.description: API requests by HTTP status code
sampleQueries.query:
{{graphQueriesTableName}}
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize Count = count() by Status
| order by Count desc

dataTypes.name: FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Insights/dataCollectionEndpoints
customs.description: Data Collection Endpoint is required for the modern push connector to receive data from the external security system.

customs.name: Microsoft.Insights/dataCollectionRules
customs.description: Data Collection Rule is required to define the schema and transformations for incoming data.

customs.name: Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments
customs.description: Role assignment is required to grant the Entra application permissions to send data to the Data Collection Rule.

customs.name: 42Crunch API Firewall requirement
customs.description: The 42Crunch API Firewall and Log Forwarder Docker containers must be deployed and running. Obtain your API Firewall token (42C_FIREWALL_TOKEN) and platform URL (42C_PLATFORM_URL) from the 42Crunch platform at https://platform.42crunch.com. Full deployment instructions are available at https://github.com/42Crunch/azure-sentinel-integration.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Create ARM Resources and Provide the Required Permissions
instructionSteps.description:
This connector receives data from external security systems that push logs to Microsoft Sentinel. The external system must be configured to send raw event data to the Microsoft Sentinel Ingestion API. Clicking on **Deploy** will trigger the creation of Log Analytics tables and a Data Collection Rule (DCR). It will then create an Entra application, link the DCR to it, and set the entered secret in the application. This setup enables data to be sent securely to the DCR using an Entra token.

[Learn more about the connector setup process](https://review.learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/create-push-codeless-connector)

parameters.content:
## Azure Resource Deployment

The deployment process will create the following resources:
- Log Analytics custom table(s)
- Data Collection Endpoint (DCE)
- Data Collection Rule (DCR)
- Entra ID Application Registration
- Role assignments for data ingestion

parameters.label: Deploy 42Crunch API Protection connector resources
parameters.applicationDisplayName: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)

parameters.content:
### Configuration Values

After deployment, copy the following values to configure your external security system:

parameters.text: Keep these values secure. You will need them to configure your external security system.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure your external system to push logs
instructionSteps.description:
Use the following parameters to configure your external security system to send logs to the workspace.

### Configuration Steps

1. Access your external security system's configuration interface.
2. Navigate to the data forwarding, integration, or SIEM settings.
3. Select **Azure Monitor** or **Microsoft Sentinel** as the destination.
4. Select **Data Collection Endpoint** as the authentication method.
5. Configure the required fields using the values from the previous step:
   - **Tenant ID**: Copy from above
   - **Application (Client) ID**: Copy from above
   - **Client Secret**: Copy from above
   - **Data Collection Endpoint**: Copy from above
   - **Data Collection Rule Immutable ID**: Copy from above
   - **Stream Name**: Copy the appropriate stream name from above
6. Save the configuration and enable/start the forwarder.

### Validation

> **Note**: Data will appear in the **FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2** table in your Log Analytics workspace within a few minutes.

To verify data is being received, run the following query in your Log Analytics workspace:

```kusto
FortyTwoCrunchAPIProtectionV2
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| take 10
```

### Troubleshooting

If data is not appearing:
- Verify the configuration values are correct
- Check that the forwarder/integration is enabled and running
- Review logs in your external security system for any errors
- Ensure network connectivity from your system to Azure
- Verify the Entra application has the correct permissions

properties.displayName: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorDefinitionName.defaultValue: 42Crunch API Protection (Push Connector via Codeless Connector Framework)

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The 42Crunch API Protection solution protects APIs by installing a microfirewall inline with the API server. Access logs from the microfirewall are emitted to Microsoft Sentinel allowing analysis and investigation of attacks.

Data Connectors: 2, Parsers: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 11

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
